decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares risk-adjustment models using observed hospital claims; no simulation-based method comparison is reported.
decisions.lineage_evidence: An empirical model comparison is not a lineage lead for simulation-based benchmarking.
named_source_mentions: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks three assembly approaches on simulated and real bacterial sequencing reads using accuracy and completeness outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-supported performance benchmark of competing methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares deterministic and CTMC epidemic models and uses numerical simulations to assess their differing predictions under identical R0.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model-performance comparison supported by numerical simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks six long-read assemblers using simulated and real pathogen reads across multiple accuracy outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-supported benchmarking study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Creates imaging benchmarks and baseline models from annotated clinical cases but reports no simulation-based comparison.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The benchmark is not simulation-based.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares nine viral-contig identification tools across simulated metagenomes and reports precision, recall, and F1 performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct simulation-based performance assessment of competing tools.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks 19 phylogenomic workflows on replicated datasets generated through simulated bacterial evolution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based workflow accuracy benchmark.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares a new incidence estimator with existing estimators in a simulation study measuring accuracy under varied conditions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The simulation study directly evaluates comparative estimator performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulated epidemic data to compare R0 estimators under well-specified and misspecified model structures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based comparison of estimation methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks assemblers on viral sequencing datasets, but the abstract does not describe simulated data or a simulation design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical benchmarking alone does not establish simulation-based lineage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks taxonomic classifiers using simulated reads with and without sequencing error, alongside real-world data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct simulation-based measurement of classification errors and performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates HLA tools by subsampling observed sequencing data rather than through a stated simulation study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit simulation-based comparison is described.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares five Rt estimation methods across forty simulation scenarios using mean squared error.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based method-performance comparison.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares denoising, clustering, classifier, and database combinations on a simulated norovirus sequencing dataset.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Simulation-based benchmarking yields accuracy comparisons and recommendations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks two workflows on observed clinical isolates without an explicit simulation component.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The empirical workflow comparison is not simulation-based lineage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares risk-adjusted models using observed admissions; no simulation-based evaluation is stated.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical hospital benchmarking is outside the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces and empirically benchmarks pyMLST on pathogen genome collections without a stated simulation design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No simulation-based comparison is explicit.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares Rt methods under different lags and windows, but the abstract does not explicitly use simulated data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The exact simulation-based design is not supported by the inspected text.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a dynamic network-SEIR simulation to compare symptomatic, random individual, and pooled testing policies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based comparison of testing-method performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks polishing pipelines on outbreak isolates without explicitly simulated data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The benchmark is empirical rather than simulation-based.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks variant callers against a validated truthset, but no simulation-based design is described.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical truthset benchmarking is not the exact simulation-based concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses observed hospital performance using composite-indicator benchmarking rather than simulation-based method comparison.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No simulation-based methodological benchmark is present.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates five outbreak-detection algorithms under varied simulated data-generating mechanisms and aberration scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct simulation-based accuracy and sensitivity-specificity benchmark.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks four metagenomic tools on simulated food microbial communities with controlled pathogen abundances.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based performance evaluation of competing pipelines.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks multiple identity-by-descent callers using population-genetic simulations and validates findings on empirical data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Simulation-based assessment quantifies bias and error across methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares sequencing protocols using a synthetic SARS-CoV-2 genome and titrated variants across performance outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A synthetic-input benchmark directly evaluates competing methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares laboratory extraction methods and validates adherence thresholds in a randomized dosing study, not a simulation study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The term benchmark refers to adherence thresholds, not simulation-based method comparison.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks machine-learning models across constructed empirical datasets without an explicit simulation design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No simulation-based methodological comparison is reported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks language models on expert-rated clinical scenarios without simulated data or a simulation study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Scenario-based clinical evaluation is not explicitly simulation-based.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares AI recommendations with observed effective regimens and specialist judgments without a simulation design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a retrospective benchmark rather than simulation-based method comparison.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks seven variant callers on simulated short-read data across genomic contexts, frequencies, and depths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct simulation-based performance and bias assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks language models on manually labeled PubMed abstracts without an explicit simulation component.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The benchmark uses curated observed text rather than simulated data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks domain-adaptation methods on public empirical datasets and repeated random seeds, not simulated data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Repeated experiments do not establish a simulation-based design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates an outbreak-detection algorithm by superimposing simulated outbreak signals on simulated baselines and measuring sensitivity and PPV.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based methodological performance evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a simulation-based model for quantifying confidence that an outbreak has ended and tests it on simulated Ebola data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and demonstration of a simulation-based quantitative framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a high-fidelity exercise to assess PPE contamination and procedural errors, not to evaluate a scientific method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a training/practice simulation rather than methodological evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses computational fluid dynamics to assess and refine a physical aerosol-protection prototype, not a methodological design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The simulation is an intervention-device application, not a lineage lead for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The abstract only states that another article was retracted and provides no methodological content.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A retraction notice is not a source for this exact simulation-evaluation concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reports empirical greenhouse sampling and laboratory quantification despite the title's simulation wording; no simulation method is described.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The abstract does not support methodological simulation lineage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses high-fidelity care simulations to trace transmission pathways, not to evaluate a scientific method's performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete workflow simulation rather than methodological evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops reporting for external empirical model evaluation but does not calibrate parameters or perform model inference.
decisions.lineage_evidence: External validation is not itself simulation-model calibration or inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes ensemble-based data assimilation to calibrate epidemiological agent-based models and estimate states and parameters.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for agent-based-model calibration and inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a neural-differential-equation method for estimating parameter probability densities in large multiagent models.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a model-parameter calibration method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulation-based calibration to test Bayesian and ABC calibration procedures for a stochastic agent-based disease model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct calibration-verification study using synthetic data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a pruning-based calibration architecture for agent-based models and tests it on synthetic and real data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development intended to improve ABM calibration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares optimization and Bayesian calibration methods against synthetic outbreaks generated by an agent-based model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation of model-calibration and parameter-inference methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a randomized survey experiment and sensitivity analysis but does not report a structural- or quantitative-bias simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit simulation-based bias diagnosis is present.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses spreading-process simulations to quantify epidemic-risk bias from nonuniformly sampled and underreported contact data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation diagnosis of bias caused by incomplete network data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates a null-effect cohort to demonstrate selection bias in observational analyses of ART timing and preterm birth.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct simulation-based quantification of selection bias.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates HIV/HSV-2 epidemics and observational studies to assess confounding, underreporting, and high-risk sampling effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Simulation explicitly diagnoses bias in observational association estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates populations and nonrandom response mechanisms to quantify bias in surveillance-derived population estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct simulation study of quantitative bias from nonrandom missingness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulation to show how mobility proxies can under- or overestimate epidemic timing and spatial spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit diagnosis of structural input-data bias in epidemic models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an illustrative epidemic simulation to quantify projection bias from imbalanced social-contact matrices.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct simulation-based diagnosis of structural contact-matrix bias.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Characterizes major-outbreak selection and deterministic-model overestimation, but the abstract does not explicitly report a simulation study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The exact simulation-based bias design is not explicit in the inspected text.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses directed acyclic graphs and simulations to identify collider, confounding, and outcome-misclassification biases in test-negative studies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based diagnosis of structural and quantitative bias.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates DAG-informed biological and behavioral scenarios to identify false vaccine-safety signals and altered detection performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Simulation directly evaluates bias and error in signal detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses simulation to evaluate proposed vaccine-effectiveness estimators under varied equi-confounding scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based estimator and bias-performance assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes the architecture and generation of synthetic Ebola datasets for a forecasting challenge.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological source for synthetic infectious-disease challenge design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes, formats, and publishes five benchmark datasets for validating phylogenomic pipelines, including one simulated dataset.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and guidance source for benchmark-dataset design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops the α-Satellite system and publicly releases generated benchmark datasets for COVID-19 risk assessment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit source introducing benchmark datasets and their associated system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a national poultry population dataset by integrating simulated farm data, remote sensing, and probabilistic modeling.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a synthetic-data methodology and resulting infectious-disease modeling resource.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Establishes an empirical Candida auris outbreak benchmark dataset for standardized phylogenomic-pipeline validation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a benchmark dataset and its validation purpose.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Generates synthetic CT images with a conditional adversarial network for training and testing COVID-19 classifiers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 application of synthetic-data generation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces an expert-labeled benchmark CT dataset and demonstrates it with three neural-network architectures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit source introducing a benchmark dataset and evaluation protocol.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Generates reference genomic and simulated metagenomic datasets for benchmarking AMR-gene analysis tools.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces and documents an open-access, curated lung-ultrasound benchmark dataset for COVID-19 analytics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes an animal-health modeling challenge and the model, surveillance strategies, and interventions used to generate synthetic data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Examines biological and measurement uncertainty in helminth models but does not explicitly adjust disease reporting or under-ascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a stochastic cattle-transmission model to estimate state-level underreporting and the uncertain H5N1 epidemic burden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates infection and wastewater processes but does not explicitly estimate or adjust reporting or ascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates hospital-acquired-organism spread across an empirical network of critical-care patient transfers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes SIS propagation in an adaptive network using pairwise ODEs, stochastic network simulation, and master equations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes an integrated travel-network framework incorporating heterogeneous cities, links, transport modes, and round trips.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses contact-network epidemic simulations to assess incomplete diary data and compare surrogate-network reconstruction methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an individual-level network framework to simulate Rift Valley fever transmission among hypothetical Kansas cattle operations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates disease transmission on an empirical physical-contact network to evaluate cyber-directed vaccination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates respiratory cross-infection across empirically observed close-proximity contacts in an emergency department.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates Rift Valley fever among cattle locations in Uganda using real livestock data in a network-based model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Models coupled vaccination behavior and infectious-disease dynamics across multiple social-network types.
